channel.title: 2026-05 on Sujith Quintelier
channel.description: Recent content in 2026-05 on Sujith Quintelier

item.title: github: Easily apply Copilot code review feedback with Copilot cloud agent
item.description:
GitHub renamed Copilot code review’s “Implement suggestion” button to “Fix with Copilot”. The updated flow now includes a UI dialog for more control when applying suggestions with the Copilot cloud agent.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Gemini 3.5 Flash is generally available for GitHub Copilot
item.description:
Gemini 3.5 Flash is now generally available in GitHub Copilot. GitHub says it is rolling out broadly and positions it as a Flash-tier model with near-Pro coding quality, but the note does not include technical details beyond that.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Expanded OIDC support for Dependabot and code scanning
item.description:
Dependabot and code scanning now support OpenID Connect (OIDC) authentication for organization-level private registries on Cloudsmith and Google Artifact Registry.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Evaluate feature rollouts with Azure App Configuration Scorecards
item.description:
Azure App Configuration has added scorecards in public preview. The feature uses telemetry to show how feature flag variants are performing in production so teams can assess rollout impact.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure NetApp Files cache volumes
item.description:
Azure NetApp Files cache volumes are now generally available. The feature adds cloud-based cache volumes that store only the most actively accessed data from an external origin volume.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Virtual Network updates – default limits increased for NSGs and route tables
item.description:
Azure Virtual Network now has higher default platform limits for NSGs and route tables. The defaults are 2,000 security rules per NSG, 6,000 addresses or ports per NSG rule, and 1,000 routes per route table.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Network Watcher rule impact analyser
item.description:
Azure Network Watcher rule impact analyzer is now generally available. It lets you assess the potential effect of NSG or security admin rule changes on live network traffic before applying them.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: ai: How to run evals for the model router
item.description:
The post explains how to evaluate the Foundry model router using an open-source GitHub repo for router-aware eval pipelines. It covers running quality, cost, and latency evaluations.

  • Source: Microsoft AI Foundry Blog

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Mock runs for Azure Storage Actions – Validate before you execute
item.description:
Azure Storage Actions now supports mock runs in general availability. This lets users simulate task execution at full scale before making any changes to Azure Blob or Data Lake Storage.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: security: Exposing Fox Tempest: A malware-signing service operation
item.description:
Microsoft says Fox Tempest is a financially motivated threat actor running a malware-signing-as-a-service operation used by other cybercriminals to help distribute malicious code, including ransomware. The service has been linked to groups such as Vanilla Tempest and Storm.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: devops+ai: 🔥The Orchestrator Pattern - Turning CLAUDE.md Into an Autonomous Agent Team Pipeline🔥
item.description:
The article presents an “orchestrator pattern” for using CLAUDE.md to coordinate multiple autonomous agents across the software delivery lifecycle. It frames the approach as a way to delegate SDLC tasks to agent pipelines.

  • Source: Let’s do DevOps

item.title: github: Start a GitHub Advanced Security trial from a risk assessment
item.description:
Eligible enterprise admins can start a GitHub Advanced Security trial directly from a Secret Protection or Code Security risk assessment. This lets them begin evaluating the products without leaving the assessment flow.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Removal of code_scanning_upload field from rate_limit API endpoint
item.description:
GitHub removed the code_scanning_upload field from the REST API rate_limit endpoint response as of May 19, 2026. The field no longer appears in the resources object.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: How Storm-2949 turned a compromised identity into a cloud-wide breach
item.description:
Microsoft describes an incident where Storm-2949 used stolen credentials to expand from an identity compromise into a cloud-wide data theft campaign without malware. The post highlights how attackers can abuse trusted systems and avoid detection.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: One-click fixes for failing Actions with Copilot cloud agent
item.description:
GitHub Actions failures can now be handed to Copilot cloud agent with one click for Copilot Business and Copilot Enterprise subscribers. The new “Fix with Copilot” button starts an automated fix flow for failed jobs.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot cloud agent: Fast, cost-efficient models for simple tasks
item.description:
GitHub Copilot cloud agent now supports additional models, letting users choose fast, cost-efficient options for simpler tasks. The change expands the set of models available when delegating work to the cloud agent.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Ask questions in context with Copilot on web
item.description:
Copilot chat on the web now opens directly on the page you’re viewing, so you can ask questions in context with less switching between pages.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Auto activation for SaaS subscriptions in Microsoft Marketplace
item.description:
Auto activation for SaaS subscriptions is now generally available in Microsoft Marketplace. When enabled, the subscription and billing cycle start as soon as the purchase flow is completed.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Application Insights Auto-instrumentation for Azure Kubernetes Service apps
item.description:
Azure Monitor Application Insights auto-instrumentation for Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) apps is now generally available. It lets you monitor applications without changing source code, using only a few configuration steps.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Front Door WebSocket
item.description:
Azure Front Door Standard and Premium now support WebSocket in general availability. The feature is enabled by default and does not require additional configuration.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: dotnet: NuGet Package Pruning: Cleaner Dependencies and Actionable Vulnerability Reports
item.description:
In .NET 10, package pruning removes platform-provided packages from the dependency graph. With transitive auditing enabled by default, projects using the new defaults see about 70% fewer transitive vulnerability reports than with the previous defaults.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: github: Audit repository Copilot cloud agent configuration via the REST API
item.description:
GitHub added a public preview REST API to get a repository’s Copilot cloud agent configuration. This lets you audit that configuration programmatically.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot Spaces API now generally available
item.description:
GitHub Copilot Spaces API is now generally available. It supports programmatic create, read, update, and delete operations for Spaces from external applications.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: How to better protect your growing business in an AI-powered world
item.description:
Microsoft Security Blog says growing businesses should use built-in security to help keep operations running, protect customer trust, and support growth in an AI-driven environment.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Remote control for Copilot CLI sessions now generally available on mobile, web, and VS Code
item.description:
Remote control for Copilot CLI sessions is now generally available on GitHub Mobile and github.com, with support also referenced for VS Code. It lets you start work in the terminal and continue managing the session from mobile or web.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GPT-5.3-Codex is now the base model for Copilot Business and Enterprise
item.description:
GPT-5.3-Codex is now the default base model for all Copilot Business and Copilot Enterprise organizations, replacing GPT-4.1. GitHub says the base model is used when an organization has not selected another model.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub App installation tokens: Per-request override header
item.description:
GitHub is rolling out a new GitHub App installation token format and is providing a per-request override header so developers can validate apps and workflows before the rollout completes.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [Launched] Update: Microsoft Foundry built-in RBAC role naming and enhancements
item.description:
Microsoft Foundry has renamed its built-in RBAC roles to match the product branding. Several role names were updated, including Azure AI Account Owner, Azure AI Owner, and Azure AI User.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: k8s: Kubernetes v1.36: New Metric for Route Sync in the Cloud Controller Manager
item.description:
Kubernetes v1.36 adds an alpha CCM route-controller metric, route_controller_route_sync_total, which counts cloud route syncs. It is meant to help validate the watch-based route reconciliation feature gate by comparing sync frequency against the existing fixed-interval loop.

  • Source: Kubernetes Official Blog

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Blob Storage SDK for Rust
item.description:
Azure Blob Storage SDK for Rust is now generally available. It supports connecting to Azure Blob Storage accounts and performing container/blob operations such as upload, download, list, and management.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: k8s: Kubernetes v1.36: Mixed Version Proxy Graduates to Beta
item.description:
Kubernetes 1.36 graduates the Mixed Version Proxy (feature gate UnknownVersionInteroperabilityProxy) to Beta and enables it by default. The feature now uses Aggregated Discovery, adds peer-aggregated discovery for unified API listings across control-plane nodes, and requires --peer-ca-file plus peer advertise settings for secure API server-to-server proxying.

  • Source: Kubernetes Official Blog

item.title: github: Grok Code Fast 1 deprecated
item.description:
GitHub deprecated Grok Code Fast 1 across all GitHub Copilot experiences on May 15, 2026. The deprecation applies to Copilot Chat, inline edits, ask and agent modes, and code completions.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot Memory supports user preferences for Pro, Pro+ users
item.description:
Copilot Memory now supports user-level preferences in early access for Copilot Pro and Pro+ users. Copilot can store stated or inferred personal preferences about how you like to work.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Timestamp fields in GitHub Projects
item.description:
GitHub Projects now has three built-in timestamp fields: Created, Updated, and Closed. These fields can be added to any project view for sorting and filtering work by when items were created, last updated, or closed.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Team-level Copilot usage metrics now available via API
item.description:
GitHub Copilot usage metrics API now includes a new user-teams report that maps each Copilot-licensed user to the teams they belong to. This can be joined with existing usage reports to analyze Copilot usage at the team level.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: k8s: Kubernetes v1.36: Deprecation and removal of Service ExternalIPs
item.description:
Kubernetes 1.36 formally deprecates Service.spec.externalIPs because the feature is insecure by default and can enable CVE-2020-8554-style abuse. Kubernetes will warn on use now, disable it in kube-proxy no earlier than v1.40 with an opt-in path, and remove opt-back-in support no earlier than v1.43.

  • Source: Kubernetes Official Blog

item.title: github: GitHub Actions: Upcoming image migrations
item.description:
GitHub Actions has announced two upcoming hosted runner image migrations. GitHub is also transitioning to own and maintain the Arm64 images for hosted runners.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Support for workloads with large files in Azure NetApp Files
item.description:
Azure NetApp Files now supports regular volumes with file sizes up to 64 TiB. This is intended for workloads that use large files, including Azure VMware Solution virtual machines with large VMDK disks.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Managed Identity Support for Azure Files SMB Is now GA
item.description:
Azure Files SMB now supports Managed Identities in general availability. Applications and services can authenticate to Azure Files without storing static credentials or account keys, using Entra-issued tokens instead.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot app is now available in technical preview
item.description:
GitHub Copilot app is available in technical preview as a GitHub-native desktop experience for starting agentic development from existing work while keeping it isolated and steerable.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: Defense in depth for autonomous AI agents
item.description:
Microsoft says autonomous AI agents require updated defense-in-depth controls centered on application-layer design, identity, and human oversight. The post frames agent security as an evolution of existing defense-in-depth practices rather than a new model.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: Kazuar: Anatomy of a nation-state botnet
item.description:
Microsoft describes Kazuar as a long-running malware family attributed to Secret Blizzard that has evolved from a backdoor into a modular peer-to-peer botnet used for espionage. The post says it is still being actively developed to support persistent, covert access to target environments.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: When configuration becomes a vulnerability: Exploitable misconfigurations in AI apps
item.description:
Microsoft Security describes how misconfigurations in cloud-native AI apps on Kubernetes can expose user interfaces, weaken authentication, and create risky defaults that lead to remote code execution and data leakage. The post frames configuration mistakes as a security issue rather than a deployment convenience.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Copilot cloud agent supports auto model selection
item.description:
Copilot cloud agent now supports Copilot auto model selection. Choosing Auto in the model picker lets Copilot pick the best available model based on system health and model availability.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Introducing Copilot CLI agent and unified sessions view in GitHub Copilot for JetBrains IDEs
item.description:
GitHub Copilot for JetBrains IDEs now includes the Copilot CLI agent and a unified sessions view. The sessions view shows live status for running and queued sessions, and the update also adds an ask… feature.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: dotnet: Process API Improvements in .NET 11
item.description:
The post describes Process API changes in .NET 11, adding higher-level process-starting APIs, safer output capture to avoid deadlocks, control over handle inheritance, lifetime management features, and a lighter API surface based on SafeProcessHandle.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: github: New enterprise installation API now in public preview
item.description:
GitHub has introduced a new Enterprise Installation API in public preview for GitHub App developers. The API fills a gap identified from community feedback and lets an app determine whether it is installed in an enterprise.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: k8s: Kubernetes v1.36: Advancing Workload-Aware Scheduling
item.description:
Kubernetes v1.36 introduces a redesigned workload-aware scheduling model with separate Workload and PodGroup APIs, a dedicated PodGroup scheduling cycle for gang scheduling, topology-aware placement, workload-aware preemption, DRA ResourceClaim support for PodGroups, and initial Job controller integration. All of these features are alpha and require specific feature gates; the Job integration currently applies only to fixed-size, Indexed Jobs.

  • Source: Kubernetes Official Blog

item.title: github: Start Copilot cloud agent tasks via the REST API
item.description:
Copilot Business and Copilot Enterprise users can now start Copilot cloud agent tasks programmatically using a new Agent tasks REST API. The feature is in public preview.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Container Apps Express
item.description:
Azure Container Apps Express is now in public preview. It is presented as a simplified way to deploy and scale applications on Azure without making infrastructure decisions.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: dotnet: .NET MAUI Moves to CoreCLR in .NET 11
item.description:
In .NET 11, .NET MAUI apps on Android, iOS, and Mac Catalyst run on CoreCLR by default. This aligns MAUI with the same runtime used across the rest of .NET.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: github: GitHub Enterprise Server 3.21 release candidate is available
item.description:
GitHub Enterprise Server 3.21 release candidate is available. The release focuses on deployment efficiency, monitoring, code security, and policy management, with organization custom properties now generally available.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Monitor dashboards with Grafana in Public, Government (Fairfax) and China
item.description:
Azure Monitor dashboards with Grafana are now generally available in Azure Public, Government (Fairfax), and China. The feature brings Grafana-based dashboard creation, editing, and sharing into the Azure Portal.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: security: Accelerating detection engineering using AI-assisted synthetic attack logs generation
item.description:
Microsoft describes research for generating realistic synthetic attack telemetry from attacker behaviors (TTPs) so detections can be tested at scale without using sensitive data.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: dotnet: .NET 11 Preview 4 is now available!
item.description:
The .NET 11 Preview 4 blog post announces the preview release and notes updates across the runtime, SDK, libraries, ASP.NET Core, .NET MAUI, C#, Entity Framework Core, and related areas. The provided excerpt does not include the specific feature or bug-fix details.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: security: Defense at AI speed: Microsoft’s new multi-model agentic security system tops leading industry benchmark
item.description:
Microsoft announced MDASH, a new multi-model agentic scanning harness for AI-powered cyber defense. The post says it topped a leading industry benchmark, but provides no technical details in the excerpt.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Copilot code review: Comment experience improvements
item.description:
GitHub updated Copilot code review comments in the new pull requests experience to make them easier to scan and act on. The changes are available to all users opted into that experience and include grouped suggestions, severity levels, and an updated comment layout.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: April reports are now available to prepare for usage-based billing
item.description:
GitHub now lets customers download April usage reports to see how Copilot activity maps to AI credits ahead of the new usage-based billing rollout. The billing unit of AI credits is going live on a staged schedule.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: dotnet: .NET and .NET Framework May 2026 servicing releases updates
item.description:
Microsoft published a May 2026 recap of servicing updates for .NET and .NET Framework. The provided text does not include specific version numbers, fixes, or security details.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: k8s: Kubernetes v1.36: PSI Metrics for Kubernetes Graduates to GA
item.description:
Kubernetes v1.36 graduates PSI metrics to GA for observing CPU, memory, and I/O pressure at node, pod, and container levels. The update also improves metric handling by suppressing zero-valued PSI output when the underlying Linux node does not support PSI.

  • Source: Kubernetes Official Blog

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Confidential computing for Azure Service Bus Premium
item.description:
Confidential computing for Azure Service Bus Premium is now generally available in Korea Central and UAE North. It lets Service Bus process messages inside hardware-based trusted execution environments (TEEs) for added data protection.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Update: 99.99% uptime for all Azure Service Bus Premium namespaces in Availability Zone regions
item.description:
Starting May 1, 2026, Azure Service Bus Premium namespaces deployed in regions with Availability Zone support will qualify for a 99.99% uptime SLA. This applies to all Premium namespaces in those regions.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: CodeQL 2.25.4 adds Swift 6.3.1 support, improvements to C# and Java, and more
item.description:
CodeQL 2.25.4 adds support for Swift 6.3.1 and includes improvements for C# and Java. The release also contains additional updates not detailed in the provided excerpt.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Virtual Network Manager rule impact analyzer
item.description:
Azure Virtual Network Manager rule impact analyzer is now generally available. It lets you simulate the effect of security admin rules on virtual networks before deploying them.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Sentinel TI - improved pattern parsing & revoke reliability
item.description:
Sentinel TI is now generally available with two updates: improved pattern parsing accuracy and a fix for revoke actions so they apply reliably. The change is meant to improve control in pattern-based workflows.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: ai: Foundry Local 1.1: Live Transcription, Embeddings, and Responses API
item.description:
Foundry Local 1.1 adds live transcription, embeddings, a Responses API, a WebGPU plugin, and download cancellation. The update expands local model capabilities and adds controls for managing downloads.

  • Source: Microsoft AI Foundry Blog

item.title: github: Synchronous SBOM API deprecated
item.description:
GitHub has deprecated the older synchronous SBOM REST API after releasing a new asynchronous SBOM API. The synchronous API is scheduled for removal on November 13, 2026, giving a six-month migration window.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: Defending consumer web properties against modern DDoS attacks
item.description:
Microsoft published guidance on defending consumer web properties against modern DDoS attacks, emphasizing layered security, resilient architecture, and graceful service degradation.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: Undermining the trust boundary: Investigating a stealthy intrusion through third-party compromise
item.description:
Microsoft Incident Response describes an intrusion that used legitimate administrative mechanisms and a third-party compromise to stay hidden. The attack avoided noisy exploits and obvious malware by abusing trusted systems already present in the environment.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: ai: What’s new in Microsoft Foundry | April 2026
item.description:
April 2026 Microsoft Foundry updates include Foundry Local GA for local AI development, GPT-5.5 support with default Tier 5 and Tier 6 quota, and new tracing, monitoring, and evaluation features for agents. The release also adds CodeAct with Hyperlight for sandboxed code execution and SDK updates for Python, JavaScript/TypeScript, .NET, and Java.

  • Source: Microsoft AI Foundry Blog

item.title: github: Cross-org Dependabot access for internal repositories
item.description:
Dependabot can now access internal repositories in other organizations within the same enterprise. This lets Dependabot resolve dependencies hosted in cross-org internal GitHub repositories.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Create repositories on the go with GitHub Mobile
item.description:
GitHub Mobile now supports creating new repositories directly in the app. On iOS, you can start from Home or Profile, tap the + button, and select Create repository.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Upcoming deprecation of Grok Code Fast 1
item.description:
GitHub will deprecate Grok Code Fast 1 across all GitHub Copilot experiences on May 15, 2026. The deprecation applies to Copilot Chat, inline edits, ask and agent modes, and code completions.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot code review comment types now in usage metrics API
item.description:
The Copilot usage metrics API now reports Copilot code review suggestions broken down by comment type. This adds a new copilot_suggestions_by_comment_type metric for analyzing code review activity.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: k8s: Kubernetes v1.36: Moving Volume Group Snapshots to GA
item.description:
Kubernetes v1.36 promotes volume group snapshots to GA, with the API graduating to groupsnapshot.storage.k8s.io/v1. The feature lets CSI-backed storage drivers take crash-consistent snapshots across multiple PVCs using label selectors and restore workloads from those grouped snapshots.

  • Source: Kubernetes Official Blog

item.title: security: Active attack: Dirty Frag Linux vulnerability expands post-compromise risk
item.description:
Dirty Frag is a newly disclosed Linux local privilege escalation vulnerability in kernel networking and memory-fragment handling components such as esp4, esp6, and rxrpc. It can escalate an unprivileged user to root and has been seen in limited in-the-wild activity; Microsoft Defender includes detection coverage.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Disable commit comments on the user level
item.description:
GitHub now lets personal account owners set commit comments to be enabled or disabled by default for their repositories. This adds a user-level default for commit comment behavior across repos they own.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: More flexible secrets and variables for Copilot cloud agent
item.description:
GitHub says Copilot cloud agent now supports more flexible handling of secrets and variables when you delegate tasks to it. The agent runs in a GitHub Actions-backed development environment, and users can pass secrets and variables into that environment.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: CodeQL 2.25.3 adds Swift 6.3 support
item.description:
CodeQL 2.25.3 has been released with support for Swift 6.3. The note does not list any other changes.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Actions concurrency groups now allow larger queues
item.description:
You can now allow multiple jobs or workflow runs to wait in the same GitHub Actions concurrency group instead of being limited to a single pending run. Previously, a concurrency…

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Upcoming deprecation of GPT-4.1
item.description:
GitHub will deprecate GPT-4.1 across all Copilot experiences on 2026-06-01. Users are directed to switch to the suggested alternative model.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Claude Sonnet 4 deprecated
item.description:
GitHub deprecated Claude Sonnet 4 across all Copilot experiences on May 6, 2026. The deprecation applies to Copilot Chat, inline edits, ask and agent modes, and code completions.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Enterprise Live Migrations is now in public preview
item.description:
Enterprise Live Migrations (ELM) is now in public preview. It lets enterprise administrators migrate repositories from GitHub Enterprise Server (GHES) to GitHub Enterprise Cloud.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: When prompts become shells: RCE vulnerabilities in AI agent frameworks
item.description:
Microsoft says new research found prompt injection issues in AI agent frameworks can lead to remote code execution. The post outlines affected areas and recommends securing agents against these flaws.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: k8s: Kubernetes v1.36: More Drivers, New Features, and the Next Era of DRA
item.description:
Kubernetes v1.36 adds multiple DRA graduations and new alpha features, including support for ResourceClaims in PodGroups, node allocatable resources, and device metadata discovery in containers. It also improves scheduling, health reporting, and resource visibility, while continuing the migration path from device plugins to DRA.

  • Source: Kubernetes Official Blog

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Premium SSD v2 Disk is now available in all three available zones in Japan West
item.description:
Azure Premium SSD v2 is now generally available in all three Availability Zones in Japan West. The disk type is positioned as a next-generation general-purpose block storage option for Azure virtual machines with sub-millisecond latency.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Dl/D/E v7 Virtual Machines
item.description:
Azure has made Dl/D/E v7 virtual machines generally available. The new general-purpose and memory-optimized VMs use Intel Xeon 6 (Granite Rapids) processors and claim up to 20% better general compute performance.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: dotnet: Copilot Studio gets faster with .NET 10 on WebAssembly
item.description:
Microsoft Copilot Studio upgraded its WebAssembly engine to .NET 10. The migration was described as straightforward, simplified deployment, and improved performance for end users.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: security: World Passkey Day: Advancing passwordless authentication
item.description:
Microsoft says it is advancing passkey adoption as a replacement for passwords, with the goals of reducing phishing risk and simplifying sign-in. The post is framed around World Passkey Day and passwordless authentication.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Rubber Duck in GitHub Copilot CLI now supports more models
item.description:
Rubber Duck in GitHub Copilot CLI now supports additional model combinations. It can use a Claude-powered critic agent in GPT sessions, with support also extended for Claude-based sessions.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Repository rulesets: User bypass and branch renaming
item.description:
GitHub repository rulesets now allow individual users to be added as bypass actors. Organization rulesets also now support renaming branches that they cover.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure SQL update for early-May 2026
item.description:
Azure SQL added a public preview update in early May 2026 for Business Critical Azure SQL Managed Instance. The change lets you right-size memory to improve performance without overprovisioning.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Enterprise-managed plugins in GitHub Copilot CLI are now in public preview
item.description:
GitHub Copilot CLI now supports enterprise-managed plugins in public preview. Enterprise admins can configure and distribute plugins to Copilot CLI users across the organization.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: Retirement: Azure Document Intelligence v3.0 API will be retired on March 30, 2029
item.description:
Azure Document Intelligence v3.0 API will be retired on March 30, 2029. Users should migrate to the latest generally available v4.0 API before that date.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Bulk Restore for Azure Virtual Machines using Azure Backup
item.description:
Azure Backup adds Bulk Restore for Azure Virtual Machines in public preview. Customers can restore up to 100 VMs in a single operation to simplify large-scale recovery.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Cosmos DB Shell—your new command line for data workflows
item.description:
Azure Cosmos DB Shell is in public preview. It adds a command-line interface for querying, exploring, and managing Cosmos DB data, with agentic capabilities powered by Model Context Protocol (MCP).

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure NetApp Files enable backup by default
item.description:
Azure NetApp Files now enables backup by default for newly created volumes in public preview. Backups are provisioned automatically at volume creation to simplify setup and improve data protection.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: k8s: Kubernetes v1.36: Server-Side Sharded List and Watch
item.description:
Kubernetes v1.36 adds server-side sharded list and watch as an alpha feature. The API server can now filter list/watch results by a shard selector so each controller replica receives only the objects in its assigned hash range, reducing duplicate network and deserialization work.

  • Source: Kubernetes Official Blog

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Cosmos DB spherical quantization for improved vector search
item.description:
Azure Cosmos DB now supports spherical quantization in public preview for vector search. It is intended to improve vector indexing speed while maintaining or improving recall and accuracy at scale.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio Code, April releases
item.description:
GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio Code changelog covers VS Code releases v1.116 through v1.119 from April and early May 2026. The post notes that Copilot can now search by meaning.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: dotnet: Durable Workflows in the Microsoft Agent Framework
item.description:
Microsoft Agent Framework now supports durable AI agent workflows using the Durable Task runtime. The post shows a progression from in-process console apps to parallel agent execution and Azure Functions hosting for serverless runs.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: security: ​​Microsoft named an overall leader in KuppingerCole Analyst’s 2026 Emerging AI Security Operations Center (SOC) report ​​
item.description:
Microsoft says KuppingerCole Analyst named it an Overall Leader and Market Leader in the 2026 Emerging AI Security Operations Center (SOC) report. The post frames automation and AI as central to future cybersecurity.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Search and filter bar for repository security advisories
item.description:
GitHub added a search bar and filters to the Security tab’s repository security advisories list. This lets you search and narrow advisories directly within a repository.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: ClickFix campaign uses fake macOS utilities lures to deliver infostealers
item.description:
Microsoft reports a ClickFix campaign targeting macOS users with fake utility fixes that prompt victims to run malicious Terminal commands. The malware is used to steal credentials, crypto wallets, and other sensitive data while bypassing traditional defenses.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Secret scanning with GitHub MCP Server is now generally available
item.description:
GitHub secret scanning in the GitHub MCP server is now generally available. It can be used with MCP-compatible AI coding agents or IDEs, including GitHub Copilot CLI.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Dependency scanning with GitHub MCP Server is in public preview
item.description:
GitHub MCP Server now supports dependency scanning in public preview. It can scan code changes for vulnerable dependencies before you commit or open a pull request.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: Retirement: Azure Reserved Virtual Machines Instances for select VM series
item.description:
Azure will retire certain Reserved VM Instance options for select VM series starting July 1, 2026. One-year purchases and renewals will stop for listed series, and the notice also indicates broader retirement of one-year and three-year reserved instance offerings for those series.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: k8s: Kubernetes v1.36: Declarative Validation Graduates to GA
item.description:
Kubernetes v1.36 moves Declarative Validation for native types to GA and makes the DeclarativeValidation feature gate enabled by default. The framework replaces handwritten validation with +k8s: markers and validation-gen, improving consistency, maintainability, ratcheting behavior, and future OpenAPI/tooling integration.

  • Source: Kubernetes Official Blog

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Application Gateway for Containers managed add-on + AKS Automatic
item.description:
Azure AKS Automatic now supports the managed Application Gateway for Containers add-on in public preview. The update removes a previous limitation that prevented Application Gateway for Containers from being used with AKS Automatic.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: AVS Support for AV64 SKU on Azure Elastic SAN
item.description:
Azure Elastic SAN datastores are now generally available with AV64 SKUs for Azure VMware Solution. This adds a higher-scale, higher-performance storage option for those deployments.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Elastic SAN support for AVS Gen2 Private Cloud
item.description:
Azure Elastic SAN support is now generally available for AVS Gen2 Private Cloud. It simplifies datastore connectivity and configuration by removing the need for an ExpressRoute gateway and using a single Private Endpoint, while also improving performance.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Single Volume Snapshots on Azure Elastic SAN
item.description:
Azure Elastic SAN now supports single volume snapshots in general availability. The feature provides incremental, point-in-time backups for individual SAN volumes, storing snapshots within the SAN.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: devops+ai: 🔥Sending Email from Claude Code with Zero Dependencies🔥
item.description:
The article describes sending email from Claude Code using only shell tools, specifically curl and awk, with no extra dependencies. It frames the approach as a lightweight, command-line workflow for composing and sending email from the terminal.

  • Source: Let’s do DevOps

item.title: github: Code-to-cloud risk visibility with Microsoft Defender for Cloud is now generally available
item.description:
GitHub says the code-to-cloud risk visibility integration with Microsoft Defender for Cloud is now generally available. The release includes follow-up improvements since public preview, bringing artifact and runtime context closer together.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Deprecation notice: code_scanning_upload field will be removed from rate_limit API endpoint
item.description:
GitHub will remove the code_scanning_upload field from the rate_limit REST API endpoint response on May 19, 2026. The notice says the field is being deprecated and will no longer be returned after that date.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: aks: Release 2026-04-28
item.description:
AKS 2026-04-28 adds new patch releases for Kubernetes 1.33–1.35, introduces several preview features, and updates multiple platform components and node images. It also highlights a security mitigation for CVE-2026-31431, deprecates Kubernetes 1.29 LTS, and starts moving Automatic clusters toward Gateway API-based ingress.

  • Source: Release notes from AKS

item.title: k8s: Kubernetes v1.36: Admission Policies That Can't Be Deleted
item.description:
Kubernetes v1.36 adds an alpha manifest-based admission control feature that loads admission webhooks and CEL policies from files on disk at API server startup. It closes the bootstrap gap and allows on-disk policies to protect API-based admission resources from deletion or modification.

  • Source: Kubernetes Official Blog

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Functions durable task scheduler Consumption SKU
item.description:
The Durable Task Scheduler Consumption SKU for Azure Functions is now generally available. It supports durable workflows and AI agent orchestrations with pay-per-use pricing and no storage, capacity planning, or idle costs.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Cleanup legacy AlternateId usage to ensure continued service
item.description:
Azure identified an issue affecting Teams Phone enablement for the Multi-line (Multiple Number Assignment) feature when customer implementations use ACS AlternateId. The guidance is to clean up legacy AlternateId usage to avoid continued service impact.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: dotnet: Microsoft Agent Framework – Building Blocks for AI Part 3
item.description:
Part 3 of the Microsoft Agent Framework series shows how to build .NET AI agents using tools, multi-turn conversations, memory, and graph-based workflows. It ties together the earlier parts into the framework’s main building blocks for agent development.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: security: Breaking the code: Multi-stage ‘code of conduct’ phishing campaign leads to AiTM token compromise
item.description:
Microsoft Defender Research documented a large-scale credential theft campaign using code-of-conduct-themed lures, a multi-step phishing chain, and legitimate email services to send authenticated messages from attacker-controlled domains. The campaign led to adversary-in-the-middle (AiTM) token compromise.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: CVE-2026-31431: Copy Fail vulnerability enables Linux root privilege escalation across cloud environments
item.description:
Microsoft says CVE-2026-31431 (“Copy Fail”) is a high-severity Linux vulnerability that can be used for root privilege escalation across cloud and Kubernetes environments. The post says a working exploit is already in the wild and urges organizations to detect and mitigate quickly.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: azure: [Launched] Update: Egress and data transfer with Azure in the United Kingdom
item.description:
Azure now offers free egress and at-cost data transfer for customers and CSP partners in the UK when transferring data between Azure and non-Azure environments over the internet. This expands an existing Europe transfer model to the United Kingdom.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Upcoming deprecation of GPT-5.2 and GPT-5.2-Codex
item.description:
GitHub says it will deprecate GPT-5.2 and GPT-5.2-Codex across GitHub Copilot experiences, including Chat, inline edits, ask and agent modes, and code completions. The notice says GPT-5.2-Codex is excepted in Copilot, but no replacement or timeline details are included in the provided text.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: k8s: Kubernetes v1.36: Pod-Level Resource Managers (Alpha)
item.description:
Kubernetes v1.36 adds Pod-Level Resource Managers as an alpha feature, extending Topology, CPU, and Memory Managers to work from pod-level resource specs instead of only per-container allocations. It is intended for mixed pods that need NUMA-aligned exclusive resources for some containers while keeping lightweight sidecars in a pod-level shared pool.

  • Source: Kubernetes Official Blog

item.title: security: Microsoft Agent 365, now generally available, expands capabilities and integrations
item.description:
Microsoft announced general availability of Agent 365 and previewed new features for discovering and managing shadow AI agents, including local agents such as OpenClaw and Claude Code.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog
